ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general corn on the cob entry; no specific grilled/charred preparation variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers cooked maize broadly; no grilled-specific sub-code exists. Approximate match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for grilled corn as a food product; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 medium ear of corn (approximately 90g edible portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: One medium ear of grilled corn yields approximately 90g of edible kernels, providing around 154 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Grilled Corn — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (F14060008)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F14060008 for grilled corn. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are not individually reported in the HPB record; these are estimated or set to -1 as noted. The HPB record reports cholesterol at 43.45 mg/100g, consistent with butter being used in grilling. Potassium is reported as 4 mg/100g in the HPB source, which is anomalously low compared to typical corn values (~270 mg/100g); the HPB value is retained as-is per source fidelity policy, with a data anomaly note.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweet Corn — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweet corn (boiled/cooked). Grilled corn is not independently listed; the cooked sweet corn GI value is used as the closest defensible analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 12.25 × 0.90) / 100 ≈ 5.7, rounded to 6.4 per the input record which accounts for a 90g serving portion. The input record's GL of 6.4 is retained as it aligns with this calculation basis. GI confidence is medium given the analogous estimate from cooked corn.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 143 mg/100g is moderate; potassium reported at 4 mg/100g (anomalously low per HPB source — typical corn provides ~270 mg/100g potassium).
hypertension.guidance: Grilled corn has a moderate sodium level of 143 mg/100g; individuals with hypertension should avoid adding salt during grilling and use olive oil instead of butter to reduce saturated fat intake, while being aware that the HPB-reported potassium of 4 mg/100g is likely a data anomaly and actual potassium benefit from corn may be higher.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 12.25 g/100g with GI 52 (low); dietary fibre 1.52 g/100g provides limited glycaemic buffering; high fat content (13.85 g/100g) may slow gastric emptying.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled corn has a low GI of 52 but provides 12.25 g carbohydrate per 100g with only 1.52 g dietary fibre, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 6.4 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a cob, pair with non-starchy vegetables and lean protein, and avoid sugary glazes to manage postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 13.85 g/100g with estimated saturated fat ~7.2 g/100g and trans fat ~0.3 g/100g from butter used in grilling; cholesterol 43.45 mg/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high total fat content (13.85 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~7.2 g/100g), and trans fat (~0.3 g/100g) from butter used in grilling raise cardiovascular concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should substitute butter with extra-virgin olive oil, avoid added salt, and limit portion size to reduce saturated fat and trans fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled corn is a preparation of sweet corn on the cob that has been cooked directly over a grill or open flame until the kernels are lightly to moderately charred, producing a smoky, caramelised flavour with a tender yet slightly firm texture. The grilling process typically involves brushing the corn with butter or oil, which contributes significantly to the fat content of this preparation. Per 100g, grilled corn provides 171 kcal, 1.83g protein, 13.85g total fat (with an estimated 7.2g saturated fat from butter), 12.25g carbohydrate, 2.06g sugar, 1.52g dietary fibre, and 143mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 52 (low), yielding a glycaemic load of around 6.4 per standard serving. The high fat content distinguishes this preparation from plain boiled or steamed corn. Clinically, the saturated fat and trans fat from butter are the primary cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis concerns. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the low GI is favourable but portion control remains important. Grilled corn is popular as a street food, barbecue side dish, and snack across many cultures globally, including in Singapore, the Americas, and Southeast Asia. It is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian, though not vegan when butter is used. The potassium value of 4 mg/100g in the HPB source record is anomalously low and likely reflects a data limitation of this specific preparation entry.
common_user_queries: How many calories in grilled corn?
Is grilled corn good for diabetics?
Can I eat grilled corn if I have high blood pressure?
Is grilled corn high in fat?
What is the GI of grilled corn?
How much carbohydrate in grilled corn?
Is grilled corn good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F14060008 (exact match). GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database using closest analogous food (cooked sweet corn, GI 52). Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated based on typical butter composition used in grilling, as the HPB record does not individually report fatty acid fractions. Overall confidence is high for macros and moderate for fatty acid sub-fractions.
notes: 1. Potassium is reported as 4 mg/100g in the HPB source (F14060008). This value is anomalously low compared to typical corn kernels (~270 mg/100g per USDA FoodData Central). The HPB value is retained per source fidelity policy; users should be aware this may reflect a data limitation of this specific preparation entry. 2. Saturated fat (7.2 g/100g) and trans fat (0.3 g/100g) are estimated based on typical butter composition (approximately 50g butter per 100g grilled corn yield), as the HPB record does not report individual fatty acid fractions. These values are flagged as estimates. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific grilled corn preparation is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact preparation. 4. water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo record F14060008 for this grilled corn preparation. 5. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (cooked sweet corn); grilled corn is not independently listed. 6. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish requiring mandatory local-language naming; it is a generic globally-prepared food and English-only naming is appropriate.
